title: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs

seo.title: Galbi: Authentic Korean BBQ Short Ribs Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Galbi (Korean BBQ Short Ribs) at home with our easy step-by-step recipe. Perfect for a delicious Korean dinner!
seo.h1: Authentic Galbi (Korean BBQ Short Ribs) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Galbi (Korean BBQ Short Ribs)?
Tips for Perfect Korean BBQ Short Ribs
seo.imageAlt: Platter of grilled authentic Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs with sesame seeds and green onions
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, sweet, and savory flavors of homemade Galbi, a classic Korean BBQ dish.

description: Galbi, or Korean BBQ Short Ribs, offers an exquisite blend of sweet, savory, and umami flavors, making it a beloved centerpiece of Korean cuisine. These tender, marinated short ribs are typically grilled to perfection, resulting in a caramelized exterior and juicy, flavorful meat that melts in your mouth. Enjoy this iconic dish at home for an unforgettable culinary experience that captures the essence of a traditional Korean barbecue.

instructions.title: Prepare the Short Ribs
instructions.text: Rinse the flanken-cut beef short ribs under cold water to remove any bone fragments. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. If the ribs are very thick, you can make small slits into the meat to help the marinade penetrate.

instructions.title: Make the Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, water, brown sugar, grated Asian pear, minced garlic, grated ginger, sesame oil, mirin, and black pepper until the sugar is fully dissolved.

instructions.title: Marinate the Ribs
instructions.text: Place the prepared short ribs into the marinade, ensuring they are fully submerged. You may need to use a large ziplock bag or a shallow dish. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to deeply infuse.

instructions.title: Preheat Grill or Pan
instructions.text: Preheat your outdoor grill to medium-high heat (about 400-450F / 200-230C) or a cast-iron grill pan on the stovetop over medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grates or pan to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Galbi
instructions.text: Remove the short ribs from the marinade, letting any excess drip off (do not wipe off too much marinade). Grill the ribs for 3-5 minutes per side, depending on thickness and desired doneness. Turn frequently to achieve even caramelization and prevent burning due to the sugar in the marinade.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, transfer the Galbi to a cutting board and let them rest for a few minutes. Slice the meat off the bone if desired, or serve whole. Garnish generously with chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds. Serve immediately with rice and various banchan.

tips: For maximum flavor and tenderness, marinate the Galbi for at least 8 hours, or up to 24 hours. The Asian pear acts as a natural tenderizer.
When grilling, keep a close eye on the ribs as the sugar in the marinade can cause them to burn quickly. Adjust heat as needed and turn frequently for even cooking and char.
Leftover Galbi can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently in a pan or microwave, but they are best fresh off the grill.
article: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs is a beloved Korean Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
